speaker: SPEAKER_00
original_text: Arriving on scene, 32-year-old male, motorcycle crash, severe bleeding from left leg.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: Scene safe, let's get gloves on and start assessment.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: Airway clear, breathing rapid, shallow, about 30 per minute.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: Pulse is weak, BP looks low, skin pale and cool.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: Massive hemorrhage from femur fracture, applying tourniquet high and tight.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: IV access established, let's start fluids, normal saline wide open.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: Prepare TXA, one gram over 10 minutes, trauma protocol.

speaker: SPEAKER_00
original_text: TXA drawn up, starting infusion now.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: Pain score looks high, let's give fentanyl 50 micrograms IV slow push.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: BP improving slightly, HR still elevated, continue fluids.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: Let's prep for rapid transport, notify trauma center ETA 10 minutes.

speaker: SPEAKER_00
original_text: Patient secured on backboard, monitor attached.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
original_text: TXA infusion ongoing, fentanyl effective, patient calmer.

speaker: SPEAKER_00
original_text: All meds documented: TXA, fentanyl, fluids.

speaker: SPEAKER_00
original_text: Loading into ambulance, continue monitoring en route.
